task:
Implement the CodeRabbit Coding Plan for issue #2230: feat: Route Mastra agent LLM calls through Bifrost gateway (#501) ## Instructions 1. `cd ~/2025slideheroes-sophie` 2. `git fetch upstream && git checkout upstream/dev && git checkout -b sophie/issue-2230` 3. Read the full issue: `gh issue view 2230 --repo slideheroes/2025slideheroes` 4. Implement according to the Coding Plan below 5. Run `pnpm format:fix && pnpm lint:fix && pnpm typecheck` before committing 6. Commit with: `git commit -m "feat: feat: Route Mastra agent LLM calls through Bifrost gateway (#501) (#2230)"` 7. Push: `git push origin sophie/issue-2230` 8. Open PR via GraphQL (same-org fork — `gh pr create` doesn't work): ```bash gh api graphql -f query=' mutation CreatePR { createPullRequest(input: { repositoryId: "R_kgDON3X_Ow" baseRefName: "dev" headRefName: "sophie/issue-2230" headRepositoryId: "R_kgDORH2m4g" title: "feat: feat: Route Mastra agent LLM calls through Bifrost gateway (#501)" body: "Closes #2230\n\nImplemented per CodeRabbit Coding Plan." }) { pullRequest { url number } } }' ``` ## CodeRabbit Coding Plan ## Coding Plan ### Summary - Implement a custom `BifrostGateway` class extending `MastraModelGateway` that routes all LLM traffic through Bifrost with Cloudflare Access authentication - Register the gateway with the Mastra singleton as the default gateway, requiring no changes to existing model routing strings - Extend RequestContext in resilient-agent-runner to carry agent metadata (name, task type, session ID) that the gateway converts to Langfuse trace headers - Maintain full compatibility with existing fallback chains by keeping circuit breakers at the model level and treating gateway as a transparent routing layer
Design Choices
Design Choice 1: Gateway registration approach: Default gateway vs. model prefix **Options Considered:** 1. Gateway prefix pattern - Requires updating all model strings in the routing table, more explicit but higher change surface 2. Default gateway configuration - Register BifrostGateway as the default, no changes to existing model strings needed **Chosen Option:** 2 **Rationale:** Default gateway approach is cleaner, requires no changes to model routing table strings, and aligns with the ticket's recommendation for "clean separation."
Design Choice 2: Metadata propagation mechanism **Options Considered:** 1. Extend RequestContext to carry header metadata and read it in gateway 2. Pass metadata directly through gateway's `resolveLanguageModel()` via custom options **Chosen Option:** 1 **Rationale:** Extend RequestContext since it's already the established pattern for passing execution context through the agent system, and the gateway can read from it during model resolution.
💡 User Tips Regenerate the plan with different choices with `@coderabbitai `. ## Implementation Steps ### Phase 1: BifrostGateway Implementation and Registration Create the custom Mastra gateway that routes all LLM traffic through Bifrost with Cloudflare Access authentication, and register it as the default gateway in the Mastra singleton.
Task 1: Create BifrostGateway Class Implement a custom gateway class that extends `MastraModelGateway` from `@mastra/core` to route LLM calls through Bifrost. - Create new file in `packages/mastra/src/gateways/bifrost-gateway.ts` - Implement `fetchProviders()` to return supported providers (openai, anthropic) - Implement `buildUrl()` to return `process.env.BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` with fallback to `https://gateway.slideheroes.com/v1` - Implement `getApiKey(modelId)` to return the appropriate provider API key based on model prefix (parse `openai/` vs `anthropic/` and return corresponding env var) - Implement `resolveLanguageModel(modelId, context)` to create an OpenAI-compatible client configured with: - Base URL from `buildUrl()` - Cloudflare Access headers (`CF-Access-Client-Id`, `CF-Access-Client-Secret`) - Langfuse metadata headers extracted from context (agent name, session ID, task type) - Follow the authentication pattern established in `packages/ai-gateway/src/enhanced-gateway-client.ts` for CF Access headers - Export gateway instance and configuration helper function
Task 2: Register BifrostGateway with Mastra Singleton Wire the gateway into the Mastra instance so all agent LLM calls route through it by default. - Update `packages/mastra/src/mastra.ts` to import and register `BifrostGateway` - Add gateway to the Mastra constructor's `gateways` configuration object - Configure as the default gateway so all model strings route through Bifrost automatically - Ensure conditional loading: only register if `BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` is set (graceful fallback to direct API calls for local dev without Bifrost)
Task 3: Update Environment Configuration Document and configure the required environment variables for Bifrost gateway integration. - Update `packages/mastra/.env.example` to add: - `BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` with default value and description - `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_ID` reference - `BIFROST_CF_ACCESS_CLIENT_SECRET` reference - Add environment variable validation in gateway initialization with clear error messages - Create `packages/mastra/src/gateways/index.ts` barrel export for clean imports
🤖 Prompt for AI agents ``` This phase implements the BifrostGateway as a custom Mastra gateway that routes all LLM traffic through the Bifrost AI Gateway with Cloudflare Access authentication, and registers it as the default gateway in the Mastra singleton. **Task 1: Create BifrostGateway Class** - Create a new file `packages/mastra/src/gateways/bifrost-gateway.ts` - Implement a class that extends `MastraModelGateway` from `@mastra/core` - Implement `fetchProviders()` method to return supported providers: openai and anthropic - Implement `buildUrl()` method that returns `process.env.BIFROST_GATEWAY_URL` with fallback to `https://gateway.slideheroes.com/v1` - Implement `getApiKey(modelId)` method that: - Parses the model prefix (`openai/` vs `anthropic/`) - Returns the corresponding environment variable API key for that provider - Implement `resolveLanguageModel(modelId, context)` method that: - Creates an OpenAI-compatible client - Configures base URL from `buildUrl()` - Adds Cloudflare Access headers: `CF-Access-Client-Id` and `CF-Access-Client-Secret` - Extracts and adds Langfuse metadata headers from context (agent name, session ID, task type) - Follows the authentication pattern from `packages/ai-gateway/src/enhanced-gateway-client.ts` - Export the gateway instance and configuration helper function **Task 2: Register BifrostGateway with Mastra Singleton** - Update `packages/mastra/src/mastra.ts` - Import the Bifrost
